title: Feed Discovery, WHATWG URL Identity, Redirects, and SSRF · Diagnostic Repair Bay
summary: A preserved TypeScript feed-service trace contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, validator, Fetch, stream, SQL, transaction, Promise, lease, Express, accessibility, migration, or release defect; find the earliest invalid transition before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in authorized TypeScript discovery policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debug…
objectives: Distinguish direct feeds from bounded HTML autodiscovery and require explicit source intent and supported media evidence.
Admit supported alternate RSS or Atom links, resolve against the final document URL, deduplicate, rank transparently, and bound candidates.
Use WHATWG URL parsing and a documented equivalence policy without collapsing query, case, port, path, fragment, or percent-encoding distinctions unsafely.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Ts Discovery Audit
steps.instruction:
Order the Feed Ts Discovery Audit Feed aggregation and TypeScript source-to-reader-and-recovery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Ts Discovery Audit.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Feed Ts Discovery Audit assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Ts Discovery Audit, keep Feed Ts Discovery Audit connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug TypeScript team handles case 7908 during an out-of-order Promise and AbortSignal rehearsal. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile direct and autodiscovery, URL identity, redirects, SSRF, and provenance; every Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, pool client, transaction, lease, and handle needs an owner; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Retain submitted, parsed, resolved, redirected, selected, and rejected URL evidence with tenant, actor, policy, and bounded causes.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Ts Discovery Audit
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-TypeScript function headed "// Evidence: feed-ts-discovery-audit-nv842n" that uses case 8685 to reconcile direct and autodiscovery, URL identity, redirects, SSRF, and provenance.
